subject: Helmets and headgear
method: full human/LLM read of transcripts 047 (Act 1) + 048 (Act 2) + 049 (Act 3), not the heuristic
note: All 56 tiers confirmed against the transcripts. The author calls helmets 'the most broken item slot' (Arcane Acuity especially), and notes the Act 3 slot is so contested that genuinely strong items land in B/C. 'Helmet' = the non-magical metallic helmet (+1 Con saves). Auto-caption renderings: Wapira's Crown='Weir's Crown', Helm of Balduran='helm of balerin', Sarevok's='sarahx', Bonespike Helmet='bones bike', Jannath's Hat='janet's hat', Shadow of Menzoberranzan='shadow of menzo barzen'.

Cap of Curing.why: Lets you spend Bardic Inspiration for a Healing Word (1d6) — a flexible, modal use of inspiration available very early, and it enables on-heal synergy builds; great for most bards.

Diadem of Arcane Synergy.why: Inflicting any condition grants Arcane Synergy: add your spellcasting modifier to ALL weapon attacks — trivial to trigger (e.g. an illithid ability-drain power) for huge bonus damage; best-in-slot for pact-of-the-blade warlocks.

Grymskull Helm.why: Critical-hit immunity (removes the damage spikes that lose runs) plus fire resistance — the main reason to take heavy-armor proficiency; lets you build a crit-immune party, with a free Hunter's Mark on top.

Haste Helm.why: Three turns of momentum (+15ft move) at combat start — a huge early mobility boost (no more dash-only turns) on any character; once-bugged but now fixed, and stays relevant for much of the game.

Holy Lance Helm.why: +1 Con saves and 1d4 radiant to enemies who miss you (no cooldown) — chains incredibly with Adamantine/radiating-orb/reverberation gear (radiant applies those), passing out many saves and stacks per turn; underrated.

The Shadespell Circlet.why: +1 spell save DC while obscured — one of the earliest save-DC boosts (the single most valuable caster stat), and you can reliably stay obscured with darkness/fog; a powerful early swing.

Circlet of Hunting.why: +1d4 to hit against Hunter's-Marked (or faerie-fire'd/guiding-bolt'd) enemies — turns the bonus action into +1d4 hit and +1d6 damage on every attack for Hunter's Mark Rangers; hit chance is king, especially for Sharpshooter.

Fistbreaker Helm.why: +1 spell save DC AND +1 initiative — the two things every caster wants, passively and unconditionally; one of the author's most-equipped items, best-in-slot for any caster not stacking Arcane Acuity.

Hat of Fire Acuity.why: Stacks of Arcane Acuity on fire damage (Scorching Ray maxes it instantly): +save DC and +spell attack per stack — a game-breaking mechanic that pushes save DCs beyond the curve so enemies can't pass; the easiest Acuity item to trigger.

Helmet of Arcane Acuity.why: Two stacks of Arcane Acuity on any WEAPON hit — trivial to trigger (any attacker), then dump four stacks into a guaranteed Command/Hypnotic Pattern via the Band of the Mystic Scoundrel; the staple of the most broken builds.

Helm of Balduran.why: Immunity to BOTH crits and stun (the two things that wreck honour runs) plus +1 AC/saves and 2 HP/turn regen (triggers on-heal items) — the premier defensive helmet; medium armor required.

Hood of the Weave.why: +2 spell save DC and spell attack rolls — basically an upgraded Fistbreaker for the late game (when you have initiative elsewhere); best-in-slot for casters not using Arcane Acuity.

Mask of Soul Perception.why: +2 initiative and +2 to attack rolls (works on spell attacks too) — two excellent, synergistic stats (go first, hit more) that almost every attacking character wants; a near-universal Act 3 pick.

Cap of Wrath.why: Two stacks of Wrath each turn you start combat at half HP (+melee damage, up to +7) — a lot of free first-turn damage for a multi-attacker, but you must deliberately sit at half HP and have a way to not heal above it.

Dark Justiciar Helm.why: +1 to all saves while obscured (medium armor) — easy to maintain and a solid all-round upgrade over the basic helmet; not exciting, but you're always happy to have it on.

Helmet of Autonomy.why: Grants Wisdom-save proficiency (if you lack it) — the most dangerous save to fail; nearly solves a build's hold-person weakness, making a physically-tanky character almost unkillable. Defensive, so not universal.

Mask of the Shapeshifter.why: At-will Disguise Self (a DLC item) — disguising your race gives better merchant prices, dialogue/quest options, and access to race-locked gear; very useful to carry even if not worn full-time.

Wapira's Crown.why: Heal 1d6 yourself whenever you heal another creature — strong with the on-heal buff set and AOE/aura healing (Ancients paladin); a lot of cumulative healing, though it lost some value when Hellrider's Pride stopped triggering off self-heals.

Warped Headband of Intellect.why: Sets Intelligence to 17 — frees a wizard-dip or Arcane Trickster from investing in Int, and it's one of the best dialogue items (with Jack of All Trades it passes most Int checks); niche but enabling.

Covert Cowl.why: -1 to your crit threshold while obscured (easy to maintain, e.g. a GOO warlock in its own darkness) — basically passive +1 crit chance, great for crit-stacking Rogues/paladins/warlocks; light armor.

Dark Justiciar Helmet.why: +1 Con saves, +1 saves vs spells, and -1 crit threshold while obscured (medium armor) — the same crit boost as the Covert Cowl plus useful defensive saves; great for Rangers shoring up their one weakness (saves).

Hat of Storm Scion's Power.why: Arcane Acuity on THUNDER damage — just as broken as the fire/weapon versions, but harder to trigger (no thunder Scorching Ray); lets you stack Acuity on a second character (e.g. with the Drakethroat Glaive or a Tempest cleric).

Assassin of Bhaal Cowl.why: +2 initiative (plus negligible see-invisibility/10ft darkvision) — a big bonus, but in Act 3 you likely have initiative sorted elsewhere, so its value is freeing another initiative item/elixir for another character.

Birthright.why: +2 Charisma (effectively uncapped, up to 26) — strong for any Charisma caster (Eldritch Blast damage, save DC, hit chance), but in the contested Act 3 slot it's often edged out by Arcane Acuity or the Fistbreaker's initiative.

Helldusk Helmet.why: Crit immunity with NO armor requirement (unique), +2 saves vs spells, and blind immunity (great in a darkness party) plus a fire spell — a huge pile of defensive stats, just outclassed for some by the Helm of Balduran or Mask of Soul Perception.

Helmet of Grit.why: An extra bonus action while below 50% HP — an extra attack is among the best damage boosts in the game (people take 3 Thief levels for it), usable by ranged builds too; you must manage sitting at half HP.

Circlet of Blasting.why: Scorching Ray at level 2 once/long rest — a handy extra damage cast to swap in when your fire-build's spell slots are strained early; duplicable by scrolls, so just a convenience.

Circlet of Psionic Revenge.why: 1d4 psychic to whatever made you succeed on a save (any save, reactive) plus +1 mental saves for gith — small damage, but the mental-save boost is a genuine defensive help for save-vulnerable martials.

Devilfoil Mask.why: Worn by 2+ nearby characters, each gets +1 Strength (and -1 to mental stats) per extra wearer — a real party-wide Strength boost for a themed all-strength party (e.g. four barbarians get +3), though Strength elixirs usually do it better.

Shadow of Menzoberranzan.why: Invisibility (2 turns) once per short rest — enough for many out-of-combat and repositioning uses, conserving your precious invisibility potions; an action, so worse than a potion, but free.

Circle of Bones.why: Free Animate Dead once/rest plus a physical-resistance aura for nearby undead — a durable zombie pet for any character (no madness downside, unlike Abyss Beckoners), and it snowballs for necromancers; arrives a bit late.

Flawed Helldusk Helmet.why: +1 Con saves and +2 saves vs spells (medium armor) — a genuinely good defensive item, but it's outcompeted in its niche by the Helmet of Autonomy (Wis saves) and the Dark Justiciar Helmet (saves + crit); fine on anyone.

Marksmanship Hat.why: +1 to ranged/thrown attack rolls — a real hit-chance bonus, but Rangers prefer the Circlet of Hunting and Rogues prefer crit-chance, so most archers who want it have a better option; never a bad pick though.

Bonespike Helmet.why: +2 intimidation and a once/turn free Menacing Attack (frighten + ~+4 damage) for barbarians — a strong lockdown/damage option that would be top-tier elsewhere, but Act 3 helmet competition is fierce.

Hat of the Sharp Caster.why: Reroll 1s/2s on spell-attack damage dice — minor for Eldritch Blast/Scorching Ray, but (via a bug) it works on Magic Missile, making it a key piece of max-damage Magic Missile builds.

Horns of the Berserker.why: +2 to hit and +2 necrotic vs non-full-HP enemies while you're hurt — a solid, near-unconditional damage/accuracy boost, but most builds prefer a defensive helmet or the Helmet of Grit's extra bonus action.

Pyroquickness Hat.why: An extra bonus action when you deal fire damage with a leveled spell (but you set yourself on fire) — great for a Quicken-spell fire sorcerer who's immune to burning (e.g. Wet), though the Hat of Fire Acuity is better for that character.

Helmet.why: The non-magical metallic helmet's +1 Con saves is actually useful very early (concentration for a cleric/Druid on Bless), so you may wear it a while before finding a magical helmet — quickly outclassed.

Key of the Ancients.why: +1 Nature (perhaps the least-rolled skill) plus an alternate solution to an Act 1 quest — wear it only for the rare nature check or the quest access.

The Lifebringer.why: 3 temp HP when you gain lightning charges — redundant with the Boots of Aid and Comfort on turn one, so only marginal in-combat regen for a charge build with no other temp-HP source.

Whispering Mask.why: See through Auntie Ethel's illusions for better loot/skips, but every turn risks turning hostile (Wis save) — usable if you pre-cast Protection from Good and Evil; only relevant for that one encounter.

Browbeaten Circlet.why: +1 spell save DC while threatened — a good effect, but it forces you into melee and is outclassed by the Shadespell Circlet, Fistbreaker, and (especially) the Arcane Acuity items available at the same time.

Coldbrim Hat.why: Two turns of Encrusted with Frost (disadvantage on Dex saves) when a spell applies a condition — useful for stacking conditions to trigger radiating orb/reverberation, but it arrives late when you have better save-DC tools.

Gibus of the Worshipful Servant.why: Charisma-modifier momentum at combat start (up to +25ft) plus advantage on concentration saves — a genuinely nice package, but spellcasters in Act 3 want save DC/initiative and already have mobility options.

Sarevok's Horned Helmet.why: Permanent -1 crit threshold and fear immunity (plus darkvision) — but the Dark Justiciar Helmet gives the same crit boost (only mildly conditional) plus saves, so this is mainly for those who'd rather not micro obscurement.

Shapeshifter Hat.why: +1 wild-shape charge (long rest) — modest extra longevity for a Druid, but most Druids want save DC/initiative to cast-then-shift; a swap-in if you run dry, not a mainstay.

Steel Watcher Helmet.why: Advantage on Con saves and blind immunity (medium armor) — lets a non-warlock fight in their own Darkness clouds, but it's the fourth-best source of that effect, only for a dedicated multi-character darkness party.

The Pointy Hat.why: Adds your Intelligence modifier to persuasion — niche, since most party faces dump Int and you've made the big checks by Act 3; mainly lets a Gale/wizard act as party face. Glorious, though.

Hat.why: A non-magical hat with no mechanical effect — pure cosmetic/superstition.

Leather Helmet.why: +1 Dex saves (light armor) — you'll have something better long before you face an enemy that makes you roll Dex saves, so it rarely matters.

Dark Justiciar Mask.why: +1 intimidation (light armor) — a tiny bonus to the least-used Charisma skill, easily gotten elsewhere; safe to forget.

Helmet of Smiting.why: Charisma-modifier temp HP when a Smite spell applies a condition (medium armor, paladin) — too hard to trigger for ~3 temp HP, which the Boots of Aid and Comfort give for free; no reason to use it.

Circlet of Mental Anguish.why: Heal 1d4 when an enemy fails a mental save vs your spell — a defensive effect that only triggers when you've basically already won the fight (hypnotic pattern, banish); too minor and self-defeating.

Hat of Uninhibited Kushigo.why: +1 spell save DC (one turn) after an unarmed hit — needs you to attack and cast the same turn, doesn't stack, and four-elements monks who'd want it lack the stats; too many hoops for a duplicable bonus.

Jannath's Hat.why: Advantage on persuasion/deception — but you've made the key checks by Act 3, and the Friends cantrip / Enhance Ability duplicate it cheaply and without risk; little impact.

Scabby Pugilist Circlet.why: +2 weapon/unarmed damage when surrounded by 2+ enemies — strictly worse than the Horns of the Berserker (near-unconditional +2 damage and +2 hit, no proficiency needed), which is itself often beaten by the Mask of Soul Perception.
